Colt Emerson LHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 52+0+0, pitcher rows 790+2380+309

Verdict

Projection: 0.310 xwOBA (-5 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

2.Jake BurgerRHBTEX vs Kyle Freeland (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.403 / xBA 0.304 / xSLG 0.487 (+88 pts)Park +26 pts14d 0.390vs LHP —K 17.3%BB 5.0%HR 8.7%Hit 28.2%Edge Hitter

Jake Burger RHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 672+1324+2089, pitcher rows 595+2490+1769

Verdict

Freeland's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.403 xwOBA (+88 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.340 -> adj 0.364); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.364 -> final 0.403); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+98 pts vs season). Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

swing length 6.2 ft
attack angle 5.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-8.6+5.0*0-3.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 4.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Zack Littell (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.430 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.590 (+115 pts)Park +21 pts14d 0.300vs RHP 0.354K 13.4%BB 10.6%HR 7.2%Hit 29.5%Strong Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Zack Littell RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 538+3015+3255, pitcher rows 670+2843+2488

Verdict

Projection: 0.430 xwOBA (+115 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.377 -> adj 0.398); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.398 -> final 0.430); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-71 pts vs season). Zack Littell is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

5.Mike TroutRHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.334 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.451 (+19 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.309vs RHP 0.293K 22.0%BB 8.7%HR 7.1%Hit 20.2%Even

Mike Trout RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 906+2511+578, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Trout should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.334 xwOBA (+19 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.334 -> final 0.334); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Trout is on a 14-day slump (-92 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

6.Elly De La CruzRHBCIN vs Aaron Nola (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.412 (+0 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.387vs RHP 0.433K 29.1%BB 4.9%HR 7.0%Hit 21.1%Even

Elly De La Cruz RHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 856+2763+2762, pitcher rows 814+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Elly De La Cruz should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (+0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

7.Casey SchmittRHBSF vs Merrill Kelly (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.326 / xBA 0.270 / xSLG 0.418 (+11 pts)Park -1 pts14d 0.303vs RHP 0.336K 24.0%BB 4.0%HR 6.9%Hit 23.0%Even

Casey Schmitt RHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+1336+374, pitcher rows 583+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Casey Schmitt should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.326 xwOBA (+11 pts vs league, Hitter). Casey Schmitt is on a 14-day slump (-55 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-106 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

swing length 7.5 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

8.Byron BuxtonRHBMIN vs Mike Burrows (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.472 (+20 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.372vs RHP 0.366K 28.0%BB 4.2%HR 6.8%Hit 20.9%Even

Byron Buxton RHB vs Mike Burrows RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 674+2052+1461, pitcher rows 836+1542+68

Verdict

Burrows' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Byron Buxton should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.333 -> adj 0.342); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.342 -> final 0.335); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

9.Junior CamineroRHBTB vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.324 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.432 (+9 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.416vs RHP 0.383K 19.9%BB 7.6%HR 6.8%Hit 19.6%Even

Junior Caminero RHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 781+2498+637, pitcher rows 882+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Junior Caminero should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.324 xwOBA (+9 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.324); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Junior Caminero is on a 14-day heater (+52 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 76.4 mph
swing length 8.4 ft
attack angle 7.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+4.3121-0.0on plane
Knuckle Curve-9.2+10.012+0.8on plane
Cutter-6.2+6.649+0.4on plane
Slider-6.9+9.2115+2.4on plane
Sinker-5.5+2.8130-2.7on plane
Changeup-6.6+12.142+5.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

11.Nathaniel LoweLHBCIN vs Aaron Nola (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.455 (+39 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.260vs RHP 0.263K 21.9%BB 10.8%HR 6.6%Hit 21.3%Slight Hitter

Nathaniel Lowe LHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 459+2640+2361, pitcher rows 814+1599+3280

Verdict

Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nathaniel Lowe is on a 14-day slump (-88 pts vs season). Aaron Nola is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Tyler StephensonRHBCIN vs Aaron Nola (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.308 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.372 (-7 pts)Park +14 pts14d 0.279vs RHP 0.243K 31.8%BB 4.6%HR 6.5%Hit 20.3%Even

Tyler Stephenson RHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 529+1424+1956, pitcher rows 814+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Stephenson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.308 xwOBA (-7 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

13.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Michael McGreevy (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.363 / xBA 0.275 / xSLG 0.512 (+48 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.409vs RHP 0.438K 17.0%BB 6.7%HR 6.5%Hit 24.9%Slight Hitter

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Michael McGreevy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 770+2148+1632, pitcher rows 783+1466+311

Verdict

Projection: 0.363 xwOBA (+48 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.367 -> adj 0.362); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.362 -> final 0.363); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball34.40.3730.383GB 33% / LD 28% / FB 38% / PU 1%0.365-90.4330.3040.64816.2
Changeup21.70.3230.314GB 53% / LD 25% / FB 19% / PU 3%0.333+100.3320.2850.44336.7
Cutter16.80.3330.312GB 31% / LD 22% / FB 38% / PU 9%0.332-10.3300.2380.48913.5
Curveball16.10.3200.323GB 45% / LD 25% / FB 25% / PU 5%0.301-190.3090.2530.39730.4
Sweeper4.60.3190.318GB 39% / LD 27% / FB 30% / PU 3%0.320+10.3230.2550.4157.9
Sinker3.90.3600.323GB 39% / LD 27% / FB 30% / PU 3%0.334-260.3420.2610.4701.5
Slider2.40.3100.325GB 39% / LD 27% / FB 30% / PU 3%0.299-110.3090.2430.39931.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

14.Miguel AmayaRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.295 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.432 (-20 pts)Park -29 pts14d —vs LHP —K 24.1%BB 8.7%HR 6.4%Hit 17.6%Even

Miguel Amaya RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 354+377+1293, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Amaya should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.295 xwOBA (-20 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.324); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+2.622-1.4on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

15.Logan O'HoppeRHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.330 / xBA 0.247 / xSLG 0.429 (+15 pts)Park -4 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.2%BB 3.8%HR 6.4%Hit 24.1%Even

Logan O'Hoppe RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 429+1758+1985, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Logan O'Hoppe should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.330 xwOBA (+15 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.319 -> final 0.330); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

16.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.500 (+39 pts)Park -25 pts14d 0.395vs RHP 0.443K 14.4%BB 13.4%HR 6.3%Hit 19.2%Slight Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 805+3241+3152, pitcher rows 802+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Shohei Ohtani should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.389 -> adj 0.363); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.363 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

17.Matt ChapmanRHBSF vs Merrill Kelly (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.347 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.440 (+32 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.202vs RHP 0.202K 25.9%BB 6.7%HR 6.2%Hit 23.5%Slight Hitter

Matt Chapman RHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 791+2213+2722, pitcher rows 583+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Chapman should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.347 xwOBA (+32 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.335); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.335 -> final 0.347); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt Chapman is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-106 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Matz's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

19.Danny JansenRHBTEX vs Kyle Freeland (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.384 / xBA 0.286 / xSLG 0.445 (+69 pts)Park +25 pts14d 0.261vs LHP —K 19.0%BB 9.1%HR 5.8%Hit 26.3%Edge Hitter

Danny Jansen RHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 436+1354+1376, pitcher rows 595+2490+1769

Verdict

Freeland's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Danny Jansen should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.384 xwOBA (+69 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +12 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.349); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.349 -> final 0.384); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

20.Oneil CruzLHBPIT vs Michael McGreevy (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.387 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.541 (+72 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.284vs RHP 0.323K 20.6%BB 7.4%HR 5.7%Hit 27.3%Edge Hitter

Oneil Cruz LHB vs Michael McGreevy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 839+2250+2404, pitcher rows 783+1466+311

Verdict

McGreevy's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Oneil Cruz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.387 xwOBA (+72 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.353 -> adj 0.376); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.376 -> final 0.387); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Oneil Cruz is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

4-Seam Fastball-5.0-0.8189-5.8swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Trent Grisham should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.314 xwOBA (-1 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.334); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.334 -> final 0.314); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Trent Grisham is on a 14-day slump (-78 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Knuckle Curve5.60.3170.314GB 37% / LD 20% / FB 34% / PU 9%0.321+40.3200.2440.44641.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 73.9 mph
swing length 7.2 ft
attack angle 9.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+3.829+0.0on plane
Split-Finger-7.0+14.19+7.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-7.1+17.76+10.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-5.6+11.717+6.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.9+9.8*4+4.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.0+9.8*0+0.8on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

23.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.465 (+27 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.474vs RHP 0.502K 25.1%BB 9.0%HR 5.5%Hit 21.2%Slight Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 737+2514+2649, pitcher rows 806+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.342); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Corbin Carroll is on a 14-day heater (+91 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-62 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

24.Colson MontgomeryLHBCWS vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.313 / xBA 0.254 / xSLG 0.418 (-2 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.315vs RHP 0.353K 25.3%BB 10.4%HR 5.3%Hit 18.4%Even

Colson Montgomery LHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 722+1088+0, pitcher rows 832+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Colson Montgomery should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.313 xwOBA (-2 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.340 -> adj 0.325); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball35.60.3030.329GB 26% / LD 22% / FB 42% / PU 10%0.319+150.3330.2210.47923.7
Sinker25.30.3230.369GB 45% / LD 32% / FB 18% / PU 5%0.296-270.3500.3130.3943.4
Changeup22.50.3250.333GB 44% / LD 18% / FB 31% / PU 7%0.285-400.3020.2480.39033.5

Curveball0.90.3080.314GB 38% / LD 25% / FB 28% / PU 8%0.294-140.2930.2410.35350.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

25.Jorge SolerRHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.458 (+23 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.250vs RHP 0.207K 22.2%BB 6.0%HR 5.2%Hit 24.0%Even

Jorge Soler RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 741+1291+2388, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorge Soler should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jorge Soler is on a 14-day slump (-57 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

26.Ben RiceLHBNYY vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.345 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.481 (+30 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.307vs RHP 0.348K 26.1%BB 8.5%HR 5.2%Hit 21.8%Slight Hitter

Ben Rice LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 753+2129+725, pitcher rows 331+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Rice should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.345 xwOBA (+30 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +28 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.345); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ben Rice is on a 14-day slump (-104 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball47.20.3740.296GB 45% / LD 19% / FB 34% / PU 2%0.359-150.3410.2660.4575.6
Split-Finger39.50.3080.270GB 36% / LD 20% / FB 35% / PU 9%0.392+830.3460.2200.50365.4
Slider13.30.3490.322GB 43% / LD 20% / FB 32% / PU 5%0.363+140.3700.2650.50146.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Jo AdellRHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.341 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.454 (+26 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.363vs RHP 0.322K 21.2%BB 3.7%HR 5.2%Hit 25.3%Slight Hitter

Jo Adell RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 772+2221+1845, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jo Adell should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.341); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jo Adell is on a 14-day heater (+47 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

30.Andy PagesRHBLAD vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.269 / xSLG 0.442 (+7 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.363vs RHP 0.387K 16.3%BB 4.0%HR 5.0%Hit 23.4%Even

Andy Pages RHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 807+2543+1810, pitcher rows 802+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Andy Pages should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

31.Mickey MoniakLHBCOL vs Jack Leiter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.427 (+38 pts)Park +27 pts14d 0.269vs RHP 0.291K 22.8%BB 7.6%HR 5.0%Hit 24.0%Slight Hitter

Mickey Moniak LHB vs Jack Leiter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 560+1602+1534, pitcher rows 847+2585+669

Verdict

Leiter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Moniak should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mickey Moniak is on a 14-day slump (-64 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.Kazuma OkamotoRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.353 (-52 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.305vs RHP 0.305K 27.3%BB 7.0%HR 5.0%Hit 16.3%Edge Pitcher

Kazuma Okamoto RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 837+0+0, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Kazuma Okamoto should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.293 -> final 0.263); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kazuma Okamoto is on a 14-day slump (-42 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

33.Shea LangeliersRHBATH vs Jack Kochanowicz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.333 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.434 (+18 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.411vs RHP 0.436K 13.6%BB 8.5%HR 5.0%Hit 21.9%Even

Shea Langeliers RHB vs Jack Kochanowicz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 731+1930+2003, pitcher rows 841+1875+863

Verdict

Kochanowicz's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Shea Langeliers should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.333 xwOBA (+18 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.355 -> adj 0.334); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.334 -> final 0.333); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jack Kochanowicz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 72.8 mph

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Mlodzinski's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Pedro Pagés should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Pedro Pagés is on a 14-day slump (-84 pts vs season). Carmen Mlodzinski is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

35.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.207 / xSLG 0.381 (-38 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.264vs RHP 0.276K 31.8%BB 4.5%HR 4.9%Hit 18.2%Slight Pitcher

Christian Walker RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 746+2519+2326, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.277); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-72 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

36.Christian YelichLHBMIL vs Edward Cabrera (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.339 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.496 (+24 pts)Park -28 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.1%BB 12.6%HR 4.9%Hit 19.9%Even

Christian Yelich LHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 302+2775+1275, pitcher rows 813+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Yelich should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.339 xwOBA (+24 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.348 -> adj 0.361); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.361 -> final 0.339); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

37.Michael HelmanRHBTEX vs Kyle Freeland (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.361 / xBA 0.273 / xSLG 0.457 (+46 pts)Park +23 pts14d —vs LHP —K 17.6%BB 4.8%HR 4.9%Hit 24.7%Slight Hitter

Michael Helman RHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 7+405+28, pitcher rows 595+2490+1769

Verdict

Freeland's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Helman should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.361 xwOBA (+46 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +12 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.316 xwOBA (+1 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+57 pts vs season). Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

39.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.247 / xSLG 0.343 (-44 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.317vs LHP —K 26.2%BB 3.3%HR 4.8%Hit 15.8%Slight Pitcher

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 715+2403+1424, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Crow-Armstrong should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

41.Zach NetoRHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.327 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.446 (+12 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.368vs RHP 0.365K 20.9%BB 4.3%HR 4.7%Hit 23.7%Even

Zach Neto RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 897+2197+2295, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Neto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.327 xwOBA (+12 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.326 -> final 0.327); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

42.Matt ShawRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.334 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.481 (+19 pts)Park -27 pts14d —vs LHP —K 23.0%BB 9.0%HR 4.7%Hit 22.0%Even

Matt Shaw RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 401+1804+0, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Projection: 0.334 xwOBA (+19 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +42 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.361); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+6.961+2.9on plane
Slurve-7.6+10.0*0+2.4on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

43.Julio RodríguezRHBSEA vs Sean Burke (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.359 / xBA 0.268 / xSLG 0.514 (+44 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.343vs RHP 0.286K 21.8%BB 4.7%HR 4.7%Hit 26.4%Slight Hitter

Julio Rodríguez RHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 839+2727+2389, pitcher rows 790+2380+309

Verdict

Projection: 0.359 xwOBA (+44 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.360); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.360 -> final 0.359); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

44.Josh LoweLHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.249 / xSLG 0.440 (+27 pts)Park +2 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.8%BB 7.4%HR 4.7%Hit 24.0%Slight Hitter

Josh Lowe LHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 495+1847+1687, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Lowe should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.331); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.331 -> final 0.342); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

45.Ketel MarteLHBAZ vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.476 (+23 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.324vs RHP 0.304K 20.7%BB 8.3%HR 4.7%Hit 23.1%Even

Ketel Marte LHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 672+2117+2175, pitcher rows 806+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Ketel Marte should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-62 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

46.Bryce HarperLHBPHI vs Andrew Abbott (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.266 / xBA 0.208 / xSLG 0.338 (-49 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.414vs LHP —K 22.8%BB 6.6%HR 4.7%Hit 17.3%Slight Pitcher

Bryce Harper LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 765+2264+2593, pitcher rows 795+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Harper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.266 xwOBA (-49 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -50 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.268); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-67 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 47.Brett BatyLHBNYM vs Zack Littell (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.343 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.443 (+28 pts)Park +14 pts14d 0.373vs RHP 0.455K 18.0%BB 6.3%HR 4.6%Hit 25.5%Slight Hitter

Brett Baty LHB vs Zack Littell RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 643+1757+681, pitcher rows 670+2843+2488

Verdict

Littell's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Brett Baty should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.343 xwOBA (+28 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.315); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.315 -> final 0.343); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brett Baty is on a 14-day heater (+61 pts vs season). Zack Littell is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Lourdes Gurriel Jr. RHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 342+1998+2089, pitcher rows 806+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Lourdes Gurriel Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.334 xwOBA (+19 pts vs league, Hitter). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-62 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball48.40.3130.343GB 39% / LD 29% / FB 29% / PU 4%0.327+140.3550.2790.4517.0
Slider17.20.3020.327GB 38% / LD 30% / FB 18% / PU 14%0.297-50.3080.2600.38523.0

Cutter16.20.3190.311GB 40% / LD 27% / FB 29% / PU 4%0.329+100.3240.2720.4356.4
Sinker1.20.3280.336GB 42% / LD 27% / FB 25% / PU 6%0.341+130.3620.2860.4936.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Cody Bellinger should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.292 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Changeup1.30.2780.318GB 48% / LD 23% / FB 24% / PU 6%0.272-60.2750.2190.35632.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 71.9 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 13.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+9.816+5.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.2+13.6*2+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-6.2+13.6*3+7.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sinker-5.5+5.818+0.4on plane
Changeup-6.6+13.6*1+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

51.Liam HicksLHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.289 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.337 (-26 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.271vs LHP —K 28.8%BB 6.6%HR 4.5%Hit 17.6%Slight Pitcher

Liam Hicks LHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 613+1537+0, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Liam Hicks should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.289 xwOBA (-26 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.287 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Liam Hicks is on a 14-day slump (-71 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Esteury Ruiz RHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 123+88+246, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Esteury Ruiz should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball45.20.3090.319GB 29% / LD 30% / FB 35% / PU 6%0.339+300.3430.2550.45714.8
Slider39.70.3160.239GB 40% / LD 21% / FB 30% / PU 8%0.311-50.2350.1750.28441.4

Sinker2.80.3140.330GB 40% / LD 25% / FB 30% / PU 6%0.321+70.3360.2600.43120.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 69.4 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 14.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-8.1+20.96+12.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sinker-5.6+7.28+1.6on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

53.Josh NaylorLHBSEA vs Sean Burke (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.337 / xBA 0.269 / xSLG 0.452 (+22 pts)Park -12 pts14d 0.402vs RHP 0.462K 14.7%BB 11.6%HR 4.5%Hit 21.8%Even

Josh Naylor LHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 666+2196+2463, pitcher rows 790+2380+309

Verdict

Burke's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Naylor should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.337 xwOBA (+22 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.339 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Josh Naylor is on a 14-day heater (+60 pts vs season). Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

54.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Merrill Kelly (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.376 / xBA 0.268 / xSLG 0.516 (+61 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.311vs RHP 0.347K 20.0%BB 11.7%HR 4.5%Hit 25.2%Edge Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 811+3102+2433, pitcher rows 583+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.376 xwOBA (+61 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.352 -> adj 0.367); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.367 -> final 0.376); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Rafael Devers is on a 14-day heater (+42 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-106 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Pitch Out0.10.3570.345GB 41% / LD 24% / FB 30% / PU 5%0.360+30.3900.2770.57622.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

55.Xavier EdwardsRHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.380 (-36 pts)Park -12 pts14d 0.332vs LHP —K 25.8%BB 6.2%HR 4.4%Hit 20.3%Slight Pitcher

Xavier Edwards RHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 798+2260+1192, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.284 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

56.Nick KurtzLHBATH vs Jack Kochanowicz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.374 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.480 (+59 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.408vs RHP 0.438K 14.9%BB 16.6%HR 4.4%Hit 20.8%Edge Hitter

Nick Kurtz LHB vs Jack Kochanowicz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 955+1965+0, pitcher rows 841+1875+863

Verdict

Projection: 0.374 xwOBA (+59 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.373 -> adj 0.364); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.364 -> final 0.374); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jack Kochanowicz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

57.Willy AdamesRHBSF vs Merrill Kelly (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.408 (+7 pts)14d 0.366vs RHP 0.384K 25.8%BB 6.2%HR 4.3%Hit 23.5%Even

Willy Adames RHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 758+2807+2804, pitcher rows 583+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Willy Adames should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Willy Adames is on a 14-day heater (+75 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-106 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

58.Michael BuschLHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.342 (-44 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.431vs LHP —K 25.3%BB 5.4%HR 4.3%Hit 17.0%Slight Pitcher

Michael Busch LHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 820+2419+2369, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+90 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball61.80.3140.311GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 21% / PU 13%0.295-190.2910.2430.3359.0
Slurve34.00.3120.298GB 50% / LD 20% / FB 22% / PU 8%0.296-150.2790.2200.34532.9

Cutter0.10.3230.301GB 46% / LD 22% / FB 20% / PU 11%0.306-160.2930.2420.35837.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Aaron Judge RHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 932+2875+3235, pitcher rows 331+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.324 xwOBA (+9 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -52 pts (raw 0.369 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.317 -> final 0.324); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

60.Riley GreeneLHBDET vs Tanner Bibee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.309 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.416 (-6 pts)Park -13 pts14d 0.385vs RHP 0.414K 27.3%BB 7.9%HR 4.3%Hit 20.8%Even

Riley Greene LHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 836+2826+2545, pitcher rows 809+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Riley Greene should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.309 xwOBA (-6 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.309); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Mike Yastrzemski LHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 586+2321+1960, pitcher rows 777+1622+175

Verdict

Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Mike Yastrzemski is on a 14-day heater (+52 pts vs season). Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+54 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). 63.George SpringerRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.432 (+16 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.222vs RHP 0.176K 21.1%BB 6.6%HR 4.3%Hit 22.9%Even

George Springer RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 484+2580+2307, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while George Springer should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. George Springer is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).
