Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 64.Jackson ChourioRHBMIL vs Edward Cabrera (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.389 (-38 pts)Park -32 pts14d 0.240vs RHP 0.235K 25.8%BB 3.9%HR 4.2%Hit 19.8%Slight Pitcher

Jackson Chourio RHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 247+2228+2163, pitcher rows 813+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.277); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jackson Chourio is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

65.Sal StewartRHBCIN vs Aaron Nola (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.292 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.384 (-23 pts)Park +5 pts14d 0.363vs RHP 0.386K 24.9%BB 4.3%HR 4.2%Hit 23.5%Even

Sal Stewart RHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 822+240+0, pitcher rows 814+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Stewart should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.292); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

66.Wilyer AbreuLHBBOS vs Michael Wacha (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.378 (-33 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.283vs RHP 0.326K 20.9%BB 6.9%HR 4.2%Hit 19.3%Slight Pitcher

Wilyer Abreu LHB vs Michael Wacha RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 767+1641+1806, pitcher rows 816+2666+2739

Verdict

Wacha's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Wilyer Abreu should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Wilyer Abreu is on a 14-day slump (-69 pts vs season). Michael Wacha is in a 14-day hot stretch (-42 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

67.Adolis GarcíaRHBPHI vs Andrew Abbott (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.417 (-21 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.262vs LHP —K 22.8%BB 7.4%HR 4.1%Hit 19.9%Even

Adolis García RHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 790+2154+2542, pitcher rows 795+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Adolis García should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.306); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adolis García is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-67 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.6 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 8.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+4.2111-0.8on plane
Changeup-6.9+13.859+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

68.Andrew BenintendiLHBCWS vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.334 / xBA 0.269 / xSLG 0.464 (+19 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.337vs RHP 0.325K 17.3%BB 10.1%HR 4.1%Hit 22.7%Even

Andrew Benintendi LHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+1729+2001, pitcher rows 832+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Benintendi should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.334 xwOBA (+19 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.342 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.334); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

69.Ian HappLHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.368 (-40 pts)Park -24 pts14d 0.346vs LHP —K 24.8%BB 5.8%HR 4.1%Hit 19.9%Slight Pitcher

Ian Happ LHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 878+2839+2728, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ian Happ should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

70.Yordan AlvarezLHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.458 (+8 pts)Park -13 pts14d 0.407vs RHP 0.391K 19.0%BB 7.0%HR 4.1%Hit 23.1%Even

Yordan Alvarez LHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 841+824+2359, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Yordan Alvarez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Yordan Alvarez is on a 14-day slump (-66 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

71.Austin WellsLHBNYY vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.300 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.407 (-15 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.185vs RHP —K 30.3%BB 8.1%HR 4.0%Hit 19.8%Even

Austin Wells LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1887+1853, pitcher rows 331+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Wells should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.291 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Austin Wells is on a 14-day slump (-102 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball46.10.3410.296GB 45% / LD 19% / FB 34% / PU 2%0.356+150.3370.2570.43510.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

72.Nolan GormanLHBSTL vs Carmen Mlodzinski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.369 (-22 pts)Park -13 pts14d 0.410vs RHP 0.377K 27.5%BB 8.4%HR 3.9%Hit 20.3%Even

Nolan Gorman LHB vs Carmen Mlodzinski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 700+1663+1601, pitcher rows 756+1648+871

Verdict

Mlodzinski's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Gorman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan Gorman is on a 14-day heater (+86 pts vs season). Carmen Mlodzinski is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

73.Victor CaratiniLHBMIN vs Mike Burrows (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.307 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.423 (-8 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.312vs RHP —K 16.2%BB 8.6%HR 3.9%Hit 21.0%Even

Victor Caratini LHB vs Mike Burrows RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 461+1418+1073, pitcher rows 836+1542+68

Verdict

Burrows' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Victor Caratini should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.307 xwOBA (-8 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.328); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball37.50.3190.330GB 32% / LD 28% / FB 30% / PU 10%0.319-00.3340.2520.4273.2
Changeup33.30.2920.286GB 46% / LD 28% / FB 17% / PU 10%0.327+350.2990.2490.37845.9

Sinker3.70.3200.324GB 39% / LD 30% / FB 23% / PU 9%0.325+40.3340.2630.4342.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

75.Ozzie AlbiesRHBATL vs Janson Junk (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.410 (-13 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.224vs RHP 0.229K 14.8%BB 2.8%HR 3.8%Hit 25.0%Even

Ozzie Albies RHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 763+2411+1686, pitcher rows 777+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ozzie Albies should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.302); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ozzie Albies is on a 14-day slump (-73 pts vs season). Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+54 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball34.90.3210.315GB 30% / LD 37% / FB 25% / PU 8%0.373+530.3730.2950.4958.4
Slider29.70.3100.319GB 52% / LD 23% / FB 18% / PU 7%0.297-130.3010.2540.38425.1
Sweeper26.40.3120.284GB 39% / LD 15% / FB 29% / PU 17%0.283-290.2520.1940.34120.7

Curveball3.00.2700.314GB 43% / LD 24% / FB 24% / PU 10%0.316+450.3140.2570.3857.8
Sinker1.40.3220.315GB 43% / LD 24% / FB 24% / PU 10%0.323+10.3230.2650.3970.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

76.Brent RookerRHBATH vs Jack Kochanowicz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.415 (+16 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.386vs RHP 0.404K 16.4%BB 8.7%HR 3.8%Hit 22.9%Even

Brent Rooker RHB vs Jack Kochanowicz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 481+2590+2391, pitcher rows 841+1875+863

Verdict

Kochanowicz's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brent Rooker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -36 pts (raw 0.360 -> adj 0.324); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brent Rooker is on a 14-day heater (+57 pts vs season). Jack Kochanowicz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

78.Seiya SuzukiRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.260 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.377 (-55 pts)Park -24 pts14d 0.243vs LHP —K 24.4%BB 12.3%HR 3.7%Hit 14.0%Edge Pitcher

Seiya Suzuki RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 637+2841+2520, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Seiya Suzuki should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.260 xwOBA (-55 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.260); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seiya Suzuki is on a 14-day slump (-87 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.0 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 4.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0-1.468-5.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slurve-7.6+4.9*1-2.7on plane
Changeup-6.9+10.943+4.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.1+5.219+0.2on plane
Sinker-4.6-0.540-5.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 81.Lawrence ButlerLHBATH vs Jack Kochanowicz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.439 (+25 pts)Park -1 pts14d 0.273vs RHP 0.277K 14.1%BB 11.6%HR 3.6%Hit 22.7%Even

Lawrence Butler LHB vs Jack Kochanowicz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 629+2521+1816, pitcher rows 841+1875+863

Verdict

Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.335); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.335 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jack Kochanowicz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

82.Lenyn SosaRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.285 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.352 (-30 pts)Park +1 pts14d —vs RHP —K 24.1%BB 2.8%HR 3.5%Hit 22.9%Slight Pitcher

Lenyn Sosa RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 363+2056+1384, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Lenyn Sosa should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.285 xwOBA (-30 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -33 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.282); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

83.Spencer TorkelsonRHBDET vs Tanner Bibee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.347 (-37 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.215vs RHP 0.238K 26.0%BB 6.9%HR 3.5%Hit 18.1%Slight Pitcher

Spencer Torkelson RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 798+2815+1716, pitcher rows 809+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Torkelson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.279); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.279 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Spencer Torkelson is on a 14-day slump (-105 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

84.Willson ContrerasRHBBOS vs Michael Wacha (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.425 (+7 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.333vs RHP 0.334K 21.2%BB 6.1%HR 3.5%Hit 21.8%Even

Willson Contreras RHB vs Michael Wacha RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 736+2163+1391, pitcher rows 816+2666+2739

Verdict

Wacha's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Willson Contreras should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Willson Contreras is on a 14-day slump (-65 pts vs season). Michael Wacha is in a 14-day hot stretch (-42 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Curveball6.30.3070.303GB 42% / LD 25% / FB 25% / PU 9%0.307-00.2950.2300.36535.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 74.0 mph
swing length 7.8 ft
attack angle 8.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.8+3.7131-2.1on plane
4-Seam Fastball-5.5+2.9184-2.6on plane
Changeup-8.5+16.836+8.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-8.1+13.3144+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-6.8+10.242+3.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.7+14.625+3.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Burke's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mitch Garver should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

86.Yoán MoncadaLHBLAA vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.312 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.401 (-3 pts)Park -5 pts14d —vs RHP —K 24.7%BB 10.2%HR 3.5%Hit 19.5%Even

Yoán Moncada LHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 532+1134+192, pitcher rows 795+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Yoán Moncada should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.312 xwOBA (-3 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

87.Randy ArozarenaRHBSEA vs Sean Burke (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.466 (+27 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.324vs RHP 0.336K 24.9%BB 7.2%HR 3.5%Hit 21.3%Slight Hitter

Randy Arozarena RHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 837+3112+2776, pitcher rows 790+2380+309

Verdict

Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.331 -> adj 0.340); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.340 -> final 0.342); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

88.Spencer HorwitzLHBPIT vs Michael McGreevy (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.337 / xBA 0.276 / xSLG 0.483 (+22 pts)Park -22 pts14d 0.366vs RHP 0.381K 12.2%BB 8.3%HR 3.5%Hit 24.7%Even

Spencer Horwitz LHB vs Michael McGreevy RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 598+1699+1588, pitcher rows 783+1466+311

Verdict

McGreevy's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Horwitz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.337 xwOBA (+22 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +12 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.358); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Spencer Horwitz is on a 14-day heater (+48 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

89.Jazz Chisholm Jr.LHBNYY vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.402 (-13 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.253vs RHP 0.306K 31.4%BB 9.2%HR 3.4%Hit 20.6%Even

Jazz Chisholm Jr. LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 774+2253+2760, pitcher rows 331+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Jazz Chisholm Jr. should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.302); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

90.Tyler SoderstromLHBATH vs Jack Kochanowicz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.365 / xBA 0.279 / xSLG 0.479 (+50 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.271vs RHP 0.277K 11.7%BB 10.7%HR 3.4%Hit 23.6%Edge Hitter

Tyler Soderstrom LHB vs Jack Kochanowicz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 703+2238+773, pitcher rows 841+1875+863

Verdict

Projection: 0.365 xwOBA (+50 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.348 -> adj 0.358); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.358 -> final 0.365); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tyler Soderstrom is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Jack Kochanowicz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

91.Marcus SemienRHBNYM vs Zack Littell (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.333 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.438 (+18 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.359vs RHP 0.383K 13.3%BB 3.6%HR 3.4%Hit 27.6%Even

Marcus Semien RHB vs Zack Littell RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 671+2076+2635, pitcher rows 670+2843+2488

Verdict

Littell's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Marcus Semien should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.333 xwOBA (+18 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.325); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Marcus Semien is on a 14-day heater (+60 pts vs season). Zack Littell is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

92.Mookie BettsRHBLAD vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.306 / xBA 0.268 / xSLG 0.423 (-9 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.376vs RHP —K 9.8%BB 7.3%HR 3.4%Hit 22.5%Even

Mookie Betts RHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 210+2796+2298, pitcher rows 802+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Mookie Betts should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.306 xwOBA (-9 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.331); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Trevor Larnach LHB vs Mike Burrows RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 518+2197+1580, pitcher rows 836+1542+68

Verdict

Burrows' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Trevor Larnach should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.310 xwOBA (-5 pts vs league, Even). Trevor Larnach is on a 14-day slump (-69 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Changeup0.10.3130.314GB 41% / LD 27% / FB 25% / PU 6%0.342+290.3410.2810.46316.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

96.Nolan ArenadoRHBAZ vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.347 / xBA 0.279 / xSLG 0.433 (+32 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.401vs RHP 0.390K 11.8%BB 9.2%HR 3.3%Hit 26.0%Slight Hitter

Nolan Arenado RHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 687+1603+2463, pitcher rows 806+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Arenado should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.347 xwOBA (+32 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.343); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Nolan Arenado is on a 14-day heater (+61 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-62 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

97.Dansby SwansonRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.454 (-13 pts)Park -32 pts14d 0.236vs LHP —K 26.9%BB 8.0%HR 3.3%Hit 21.7%Even

Dansby Swanson RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 743+2625+2365, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Dansby Swanson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). Dansby Swanson is on a 14-day slump (-76 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+9.371+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Coby Mayo RHB vs Steven Matz LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 546+1133+188, pitcher rows 601+1197+758

Verdict

Matz's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Coby Mayo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.320 xwOBA (+5 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +19 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.337); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Coby Mayo is on a 14-day heater (+42 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

99.Ryan McMahonLHBNYY vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.286 / xBA 0.209 / xSLG 0.383 (-29 pts)Park -12 pts14d 0.230vs RHP —K 33.9%BB 9.2%HR 3.3%Hit 18.9%Slight Pitcher

Ryan McMahon LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 523+2572+2741, pitcher rows 331+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan McMahon should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.286); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryan McMahon is on a 14-day slump (-49 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball45.70.3510.296GB 45% / LD 19% / FB 34% / PU 2%0.336-160.3170.2360.41112.2
Split-Finger41.40.2990.270GB 36% / LD 20% / FB 35% / PU 9%0.301+20.2560.1770.35366.2
Slider12.90.3100.322GB 43% / LD 20% / FB 32% / PU 5%0.282-280.2880.2120.37963.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
